Shell.tagline: Volunteer, internship and Spanish experiences with real impact.
Shell.footer: Bilingual architectural base prepared to grow the MVP inside a single Next.js application.

AdminShell.notice: Administrative area prepared at the routing and layout level. Authentication will arrive in a later phase with Auth.js.

login.title: Admin login
login.description: Reserved screen for MVP administrative access. In a later phase it will connect to Auth.js and Google OAuth.
sections: Keeps admin entry separate from the public site.
Allows the MVP to anticipate access states and security messaging.
Authentication is still intentionally out of scope at this stage.

dashboard.title: Admin dashboard
dashboard.description: Operational summary to monitor programs, applications, reports and general configuration from one administrative base.
sections: This will become the protected entry point for the internal team.
It can later consolidate MVP metrics and early alerts.
It is already connected to the shared admin layout.

programs.title: Programs management
programs.description: Administrative listing to create, edit, publish or unpublish programs according to the functional map defined in the master document.
sections: Prepared for program CRUD.
Compatible with slugs, statuses and bilingual content.
It will connect to models and services in a later iteration.

programsNew.title: Create program
programsNew.description: Future form to register new volunteering, internship or Spanish programs from the internal panel.
sections: It should include short content, extended content and SEO.
It is aligned with the programs model from the master document.
It still works as an architectural placeholder.

programsEdit.title: Edit program
programsEdit.description: Dynamic route prepared to update an existing program through its internal identifier.
sections: The [id]/edit structure is already defined.
It will support editing, publishing and later auditing.
It is the administrative counterpart of the public slug detail.

applications.title: Applications inbox
applications.description: Applications queue to review submissions, filter them by status and coordinate internal follow-up.
sections: Designed to support process states and traceability.
It will connect to validation and persistence later on.
It is a central piece of the operational MVP.

applicationDetail.title: Application detail
applicationDetail.description: Individual view to inspect the applicant information, their program of interest and the management history.
sections: The dynamic [id] route already exists.
It will support status updates and consent review.
It can evolve into contextual logs and internal notes.

reports.title: Reports
reports.description: Area reserved for basic MVP reports, with future CSV export and summarized visualizations.
sections: Aligned with functional requirement RF-09.
It will be ideal for program and application metrics.
It is ready to grow without mixing responsibilities.

activity.title: Activity log
activity.description: Section to register relevant administrative actions and maintain a basic operational log.
sections: It responds to functional requirement RF-10.
It can later rely on activity_logs.
It helps provide traceability to the internal panel.

settings.title: Site settings
settings.description: Area for branding, contact data, social links, impact graphics and site notification emails.
sections: Related to site_settings in the data model.
It will help avoid scattered hardcodes.
The architecture already separates this responsibility into a dedicated route.

catalogSize.label: Catalog size
catalogSize.description: Single source of truth for admin and public routes.

publishedNow.label: Published now
publishedNow.description: Only published items appear in the localized public catalog.

featuredHighlights.label: Featured highlights
featuredHighlights.description: Prepared for curated promotion on marketing surfaces.

table.heading: Programs ready for future CRUD
table.description: This view already reflects category, publication state, featured flag and edit entry points.

intro.create: This screen mirrors the future program model so the CRUD form can land without redefining the domain contract.
intro.edit: This screen already loads the program entity from the shared service layer. Next iteration only needs real form state, submit actions and persistence.

requirements.0: Capture a structured list per locale.
requirements.1: Reuse the shared validator before persistence exists.
requirements.2: Keep compatibility with future form arrays.

included.1: Support details that will later sync to public detail pages.
included.2: A future save action connected to MongoDB/Mongoose.

AdminProgramForm.seoAuditDescription: SEO titles/descriptions, createdBy, updatedBy and timestamps are already part of the entity. Persistence and auth can plug into these fields without reshaping the model.

placeholders.shortDescription: Short description for cards and lists

NotFound.title: Page not found
NotFound.description: The requested route does not exist or has not been implemented in the MVP yet.

home.title: We connect people with intercultural programs in Costa Rica.
home.description: This base introduces the organization, its programs and the initial funnel to attract international volunteers from a single fullstack application.
home.highlightOne: Clear promotion of volunteering, internships and Spanish classes.
home.highlightTwo: Architecture ready for forms, email and database integration.
home.highlightThree: Public routing cleanly separated from the admin panel.

about.title: About Pura Vida Interculturas
about.description: Institutional space to communicate purpose, local experience and the international value proposition of the organization.
about.highlightOne: Content structure aimed at strengthening international visibility.
about.highlightTwo: Ready for SEO, bilingual content and institutional storytelling.
about.highlightThree: Ideal for mission, vision, history and team content in upcoming iterations.

programs.title: Available programs
programs.description: Initial listing for volunteering, internships and Spanish classes with room for filters and dynamic publishing.
programs.highlightOne: Prepared for categories, publication status and featured programs.
programs.highlightTwo: Compatible with per-program slugs and SEO metadata.
programs.highlightThree: Ready to connect to MongoDB once persistence is implemented.

programDetail.title: Program detail view
programDetail.description: Reserved screen to show full description, location, duration, requirements and inclusions for each program.
programDetail.highlightOne: The dynamic URL uses a slug for future indexable pages.
programDetail.highlightTwo: It will consume bilingual content from translations.es and translations.en.
programDetail.highlightThree: This is the natural handoff point to the application CTA.

apply.title: Apply to a program
apply.description: Prepared screen for the open MVP application form with privacy consent and only the minimum required data.
apply.highlightOne: Later it will capture full name, email, country, start date and message.
apply.highlightTwo: The architecture already expects centralized client/server validation.
apply.highlightThree: After submission, the flow leads to a dedicated success page.

applySuccess.title: Application received
applySuccess.description: Success page meant to acknowledge the submission and prepare future automated email confirmations.
applySuccess.highlightOne: Builds trust immediately after form submission.
applySuccess.highlightTwo: Useful later as an analytics conversion touchpoint.
applySuccess.highlightThree: Can evolve into personalized next-step guidance.

impact.title: Impact and resource allocation
impact.description: Section intended to communicate outcomes, resource use and visual representation of institutional impact.
impact.highlightOne: Aligned with the requirement to display impact or resource distribution.
impact.highlightTwo: Prepared for future charts powered by Recharts.
impact.highlightThree: Supports trust for an international audience.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: Screen to centralize communication channels, location details and future inbound contact messages.
contact.highlightOne: Can evolve into a contact form and richer institutional data.
contact.highlightTwo: Keeps the funnel open without requiring user accounts.
contact.highlightThree: Helps resolve questions before somebody starts an application.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.description: Initial base to document explicit consent, minimum data handling and compliance around the application flow.
privacy.highlightOne: Essential for the consent required in the application form.
privacy.highlightTwo: Acts as the anchor for future privacy versioning.
privacy.highlightThree: Will connect to validation and consent tracking in applications.

ProgramsUi.empty: There are no published programs yet. The admin catalog is already prepared for the next iteration.
